meta.notes: Demo dataset for seeding a student productivity assistant (tasks, mood, sessions, prefs, chat history).

personalNotes: Tends to overestimate what fits into one evening; prefers 2–3 core tasks/day.
Responds well to 'start with 2 minutes' prompts when stuck.
Best focus late morning; avoid heavy work after 22:30.

chatHistory.content: I’m behind on my report and feeling stressed.
Let’s triage: what’s due soonest and what’s the minimum viable submission?
FYP evaluation and testing section due next week.
Okay—let’s create 3 focused blocks: test plan, metrics, then write-up. Want 25 or 50-minute sprints?
25 minutes.
Great. Start with the test plan: list 5 core scenarios and expected results.
Also I keep procrastinating.
Shrink it: open the document and write just the heading + 3 bullet points. Two minutes. Go.

knowledgeBaseSeeds.title: Study Methods Cheatsheet
knowledgeBaseSeeds.text:
Pomodoro: 25 min focus + 5 min break; after 4 rounds, take a 15–30 min break.
80/20: starting is the hardest part—reduce friction with a 2-minute starter step.
Time-blocking: pick 1–3 MITs (Most Important Tasks) per day and schedule them.
Active recall + spaced repetition beats rereading; use quick self-tests.
If stuck: write an outline with headings, then fill sections in short passes.

knowledgeBaseSeeds.title: FYP Writing Guidance
knowledgeBaseSeeds.text:
Evaluation: define metrics (task completion rate, perceived usefulness, SUS score).
Testing: include unit tests, API tests (Postman), and usability testing with questionnaires.
Write-up structure: intro, design, implementation, testing, evaluation, conclusion.
Always link features to requirements and evidence (screenshots, logs, user feedback).
